surface.name: Confluent Cloud Console quick start, Section 1
surface.selection_basis: Confluent Cloud's overview sends new users to the Cloud Quick Start, whose first section explicitly gets a Basic Kafka cluster running, creates a topic, produces data, and displays the resulting messages. The Cloud Console is the first tab shown and avoids adding the CLI installation gate.

alternatives_considered.name: Confluent CLI route
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This requires local CLI version 3.0 or later and extra operating-system tooling.

alternatives_considered.name: Confluent Platform quick start
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is the separate self-managed distribution; the cloud overview directly recommends the Confluent Cloud Quick Start.

entry_point.developer_intent: Stream sample data through a first Confluent Cloud Kafka topic and see the messages.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived at the official Confluent Cloud Quick Start.
The developer has no Kafka cluster, topic, or sample producer yet.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A Basic Confluent Cloud Kafka cluster is running, a users topic exists, a Datagen connector is producing sample records, and those records are visible in the topic's Messages tab.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The connector status changes from Provisioning to Running, then the users topic's Messages tab displays the produced records.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Section 1 says it sets up a cluster and produces data; Step 4 states the users topic is receiving messages and directs the user to view them.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: It is the first externally observable streaming-data result in the vendor's official Cloud Quick Start. Stream Lineage and Flink SQL follow afterward.

prerequisites.requirement: A Confluent Cloud account is required. Signup supports local email and password, Google, or GitHub; local signup collects full name, organization, email, country, and terms acceptance, then verifies email and creates a password.
A working Confluent Cloud environment with a Stream Governance package and internet connectivity are assumed.

primary_path.action: Create a Confluent Cloud account with local email and password, Google, or GitHub, then enter the required profile and country details and accept the applicable terms.
details: The local route collects full name, organization, email, and country, then uses Start free, an emailed verification link, and a password-creation page.
Google and GitHub routes complete identity-provider authentication, then collect full name, organization, and country before Submit.
Confluent's try-free form requests a business email for personalized technical assistance and presents Terms of Service acceptance.
primary_path.input: Identity-provider account or local signup details, country, organization, and terms acceptance.
primary_path.output: Authenticated Confluent Cloud Console and automatically credited organization.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer is signed in to the Cloud Console and the organization receives its trial credit.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The local route waits for email verification. The docs do not state an email-delivery SLA or a general manual approval step.

primary_path.action: Sign in to Confluent Cloud and click Add cluster.
details: The tutorial creates the cluster inside the default environment.
primary_path.input: Confluent Cloud account and environment access.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A free-trial signup is required if access is absent.

primary_path.action: Select Begin configuration for Basic, choose cloud provider, region, and one availability zone, then continue.
details: Basic is the cluster type used by the quick start.
primary_path.success_signal: The Create cluster page shows configuration and cost information.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If no payment method exists, the Set payment page appears and must be completed.

primary_path.action: Name the cluster, review cost information, and select Launch cluster.
details: Provisioning may take a few minutes.

primary_path.action: Open Topics, click Create topic, enter `users`, and select Create with defaults.
details: The quick start defines a topic as the unit of organization for the cluster.
primary_path.output: Users topic available to producers and consumers.
primary_path.success_signal: A success message confirms topic creation.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Ignore any extra action suggested by the success message and continue to the producer step.

primary_path.action: Configure the Datagen Source connector for the users topic.
details: Open Connectors, search for datagen, select Datagen Source, choose the Users template and users topic.
Select My account, generate and download a Kafka API key and secret, then choose JSON_SR, one task, and the documented connector name.
primary_path.input: Users topic, generated cluster API credentials, JSON_SR format, and connector name.
primary_path.success_signal: Connector status changes from Provisioning to Running.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Provisioning lasts several seconds; the secret is downloaded and should be retained.

primary_path.action: Open Topics, select users, and open its Messages tab.
details: The page states the users topic is now receiving messages.
primary_path.output: Visible stream of generated users records.
primary_path.success_signal: Messages are displayed in the topic detail page.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the connector is not Running, messages may not yet appear.

branches.condition: The developer chooses CLI or REST rather than Cloud Console.
branches.path: Use the parallel tabs to log in, create a cluster and topic, generate the required API credentials, define the Datagen connector configuration, and create the connector through commands or APIs.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The same running producer is reached, but local CLI installation or Cloud and Kafka API authentication are added.

friction_gates.description: Confluent Cloud signup requires a local email route or Google or GitHub identity, plus profile and country details; local signup adds email verification and password creation.

The organization automatically receives $400 in credit for up to 30 days. The trial ends when credit is exhausted or expires, and the organization is suspended if no payment or new promotion is available.
Cluster creation displays cost information and requires setting a payment method when none exists; created resources can be billable.
Cloud provider, region, and availability zone must be selected.
Cluster provisioning can take a few minutes.
The connector needs a generated Kafka API key and secret, which are downloaded during setup.

sources.title: Quick Start for Confluent Cloud
evidence_supported: Account, governance, billing, choice, credential, and wait gates
Running connector and visible-message completion signals

sources.title: Overview of Confluent Cloud Tutorials and Examples
evidence_supported: Vendor routing to Cloud Quick Start

Local user: Sign in with GitHub

sources.title: FAQ for Confluent Cloud Billing

sources.title: Start for free on Confluent Cloud
evidence_supported: Full name, company, and country fields

uncertainties.question: Does an existing default environment always include an eligible Stream Governance package for new accounts?
uncertainties.impact: A missing package adds an environment configuration or upgrade gate before cluster work.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The quick start assumes a working environment with a package but does not guarantee every account's initial state.

excluded_after_success: Production networking, IAM, scaling, and monitoring
Resource cleanup, although the docs recommend it to avoid charges
